originalTitle: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
title: Seven Football Friends Stay Behind After Big Game!
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with an important story. It's about some brave football players from a country called Eritrea. They went to play a big football game for their country, which is very exciting! But when it was time for the team to go home, seven of these players did not get on the bus. They decided to stay in the other country. This happened after their international match in a country called Uganda.

What Happened to Our Football Friends?

Imagine you go on a fun school trip with your sports team. You play a great game, laugh with your friends, and have a super time! Then, when the trip is over, everyone gets on the bus to go back home. But seven players from the Eritrean national football team did not get on their bus. They chose to stay in Uganda instead of going back to Eritrea. It was a very big decision for them. These players are part of their country's national team. That means they are some of the best players, chosen to play for their whole country! It is a huge honor to wear your country's colors. **Did you know?** A national team wears special jerseys with their country's flag or colors on them! It's like wearing a superhero cape for your country.

Playing for Your Country!

Playing football for your country is a giant dream for many kids all over Africa. You train so hard every day. You learn amazing skills like passing the ball, dribbling, and scoring incredible goals. When you play for your country, you often get to travel to new places. You see different cities, maybe even different countries! You meet new people and learn about their homes. It's a very exciting adventure! These seven players loved football very much. They were good at it, and they made their country proud on the football field. But sometimes, people have even bigger wishes for their lives. Sometimes, people want to try new things in a new place. And sometimes, people look for a safer place to live, or a place where they feel they can be more free. **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in Africa! Many children everywhere dream of becoming a famous footballer and playing in big stadiums.

Eritrea: A Land Far Away

Eritrea is a country right here in Africa. It is in the eastern part of our big continent, close to the Red Sea. It has beautiful mountains and lovely beaches. Eritrea is a place with its own special food, music, and traditions. People there speak a language called Tigrinya, which sounds very interesting! But for some people in Eritrea, life can be very, very hard. Some grown-ups say it is difficult to make your own choices there. It can be hard to decide what job you want to do, or where you want to live. **Did you know?** Eritrea is home to many ancient historical sites! Some buildings and places are thousands of years old, full of amazing stories from the past.

Why Did They Not Go Back?

This part of the story can be a little tricky to understand for young minds, but we will make it simple. When we say someone "did not go back," it means they chose to leave their team and stay in the new country. Why would someone make such a big choice? Maybe they felt worried about what would happen if they went home. Maybe they wished for a different kind of life. They might want to live in a place where they feel more free to make their own decisions. A place where they can choose their own future and chase their own dreams. Perhaps they want to play football in a country where they can earn more money to help their families. Or maybe they simply feel safer and happier in a new place. They might be looking for a new start, like a fresh page in a storybook. A chance to build a different kind of life for themselves, with more choices and opportunities. **Did you know?** People who leave their country to find safety or a better life are sometimes called "refugees" or "asylum seekers." They are often looking for a new, peaceful home. It's like when you really want to choose your favorite toy to play with, but someone else always picks it for you. You would want to be able to choose for yourself, right? These players wanted to choose their own lives and make their own big choices.

It's Happened Before...

This is not the first time something like this has happened with players from Eritrea. Many other Eritrean football players have done the same thing in the past. Over many years, several players have left their teams when they traveled to other countries for games. They did not return home to Eritrea with their teammates. This tells us that many players from Eritrea might feel the same way. They might all be wishing for a life with more freedom, more choices, and more opportunities to be happy. **Did you know?** Football teams travel all around the world for games! Sometimes they go to very cold places where it snows, and other times to very hot places near the desert!

Everyone Deserves to Feel Safe

It is super important for everyone in the world to feel safe and happy. Everyone should feel like they can make their own choices about their life and their future. Every child, every grown-up, every person deserves to be happy. They deserve to be free to follow their dreams, whatever those dreams might be. These seven players chose to stay in Uganda because they hoped for a better life. They hoped for more freedom, more chances, and more safety. We hope they find exactly what they are looking for. We hope they find a safe place where they can live happily. And we hope they can keep playing football and enjoying their favorite sport. **Did you know?** There is something called the Universal Declaration of Human Rights that says everyone has the right to freedom and safety. It's like a rulebook for how we should all treat each other!

What Happens Next?

Now, these seven players will try to build new lives for themselves in Uganda. They might try to find a new football team to play for, or they might look for different jobs. They will learn all about their new home in Uganda. It will be a big change for them, and it might be a little scary, but they are brave. They want a chance to live freely and happily. We send them lots of good wishes from all of us here in Africa. We hope they find success and happiness in their new journey. They are still our football friends, even if they are far away. And we wish them all the very best! **Did you know?** Many African countries welcome people who are looking for a new home and safety. It shows we care about each other and want everyone to be well.

Let's Send Them Good Wishes!

So, dear kids, remember our seven football friends from Eritrea. They made a very big decision, a brave decision. They chose to follow their dreams and find a life where they feel more free. It is good to dream big dreams. It is good to want to be free and happy. Let's hope they find peace and joy in their new journey. Let's cheer them on, even from afar, with all our hearts!
summary: Seven football players from Eritrea decided not to return home after a match in Uganda, hoping to find a safer and freer life in a new country.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows how important it is for everyone to feel safe and free to make their own choices and follow their dreams, even if it means making very big decisions to find a new home.

originalTitle: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
content: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.

originalTitle: Churches and politicians in South Sudan call for 'lasting peace' in Easter messages
title: Let's Play Nicely: Friends Want Peace in South Sudan!
content:
Hello, amazing young reporters! Welcome to our special news report from a beautiful country in Africa called South Sudan. Imagine a place where children love to play, learn, and grow, just like you! But sometimes, in some places, people can get sad or scared if there is fighting. Our news today is about how grown-ups and friends in South Sudan are working hard to make sure everyone can live happily and safely, always.

A Special Place Called South Sudan

South Sudan is a country far away, but it's part of our big African family. It has pretty rivers, big trees, and lots of different people with wonderful stories and songs. It's a land full of sunshine and hope. Just like your town or village has different families and friends, South Sudan has many groups of people. And guess what? All these people want the same thing: to live in peace. Did you know? South Sudan is one of the newest countries in the world! It became its own country in the year 2011. That's not so long ago, right?

What Does "Peace" Really Mean?

Peace is a very special word. It means everyone is happy and safe. It means no fighting, no loud shouts, and no one being scared. Imagine playing with your friends without arguing over a toy. Imagine your mommy and daddy smiling all the time. That’s what peace feels like! It means being kind to each other, listening to each other, and sharing. When there is peace, children can go to school every day, learn new things, and play outside with their friends.

Voices for Peace: Churches and Leaders Speak Out

In South Sudan, many important grown-ups are talking about peace right now. Who are these grown-ups? Well, imagine your big brother or sister, or your favorite teacher, or even the elder in your community. They are like the leaders and helpers. The people in churches are speaking very loudly about peace. Churches are places where people go to pray, sing, and learn about being good. They are like big families that want everyone to be happy and kind to one another. During a special time called Easter – which is a time for hope and new beginnings for many people – they sent out messages asking everyone to be friends. They said, 'Let’s hold hands and work together!' Did you know? There are many different churches in South Sudan, and they all teach people to love and care for each other.

Why is Peace So Super Important for Everyone?

Peace is like sunshine for a flower; it helps everything grow beautifully! When there is peace, wonderful things happen. **For Children:** You can go to school and learn your ABCs and 123s. You can draw colorful pictures and read exciting stories. You can play soccer, skip rope, or chase butterflies with your friends without any worries. You can sleep soundly at night. **For Families:** Moms and dads can go to work safely to earn money for food and clothes. Families can eat meals together, laugh, and tell stories. No one has to hide or be afraid. **For the Country:** Farmers can grow yummy food in their fields without being scared. Doctors and nurses can help sick people in hospitals. Shops can sell groceries and toys. Everything just works better and makes everyone happy! Did you know? South Sudan has big rivers, like the Nile, where people can fish and get water for their farms. Peace helps keep these important places safe for everyone to use!

No More Fighting! How People are Helping

Sometimes, in the past, there was fighting in South Sudan. And fighting makes everyone sad and scared. It means children can't go to school, and families might have to leave their homes. No one wants that to happen again, especially not the children. That's why these church leaders and other important people are asking everyone to stop, listen, and talk. They want people to share their ideas and solve problems with words, not with shouts or fights. It’s like when you and your friend have a disagreement; instead of pushing, you talk about it and find a way to play together again. They are asking all grown-ups to be good friends to each other, just like you are good friends on the playground. Did you know? South Sudan has many different groups of people, and each group has its own special songs and dances! Peace helps everyone share their beautiful cultures without fear.

You Can Help Too!

Even as a child, you can help make peace! How? By being kind to your friends, by sharing your toys, by listening to your teachers, and by helping your family. Every time you choose kindness over fighting, you are helping to build a peaceful world. The people in South Sudan want a future full of smiles, laughter, and friendship for all children. Let's send them our good wishes and hope that their calls for lasting peace make South Sudan an even happier and safer home for everyone, always.
summary: Important leaders and churches in South Sudan are asking everyone to work together for peace so children can be safe and happy, and no one has to fight.
whyMatters: This is important because peace means everyone can live safely, go to school, play with friends, and families can be happy without any fighting or worries.

originalTitle: North Korea keeping Iran at arm’s length, reports Seoul
title: North Korea Wants to Chat, Being Careful!
content:
Hello, hello, my wonderful friends! It’s me, your favorite news reporter, here to tell you amazing stories from our big, big world. Today, we’re going on a little adventure to a place far, far away. We are going to talk about a country called North Korea and how it wants to make friends and talk to another big country, the USA.

What is a Country?

Imagine your home. It’s a special place, right? It has rules, and your family lives there. A country is like a very, very big home for many, many families! Each country has its own special name, its own leaders who help make rules, and its own special places. Our world has so many countries, like our own African countries, and countries far away. Each one is unique and important. They all share the same big Earth, and sometimes, they like to talk to each other, just like you talk to your neighbors or friends.

Meet North Korea!

Now, let's zoom across the oceans to a country called North Korea. It’s in a part of the world called Asia. It’s a land with big mountains and sometimes it gets very cold there. The people in North Korea have their own way of life, their own special foods, and their own stories. Did you know? North Korea is on a peninsula! That means it’s a piece of land with water on three sides, almost like a big finger pointing out into the sea!

Meet South Korea, the Neighbor!

Right next door to North Korea is another country called South Korea. They are like neighbors living in houses right next to each other. Sometimes neighbors are very close friends, and sometimes they just live peacefully side by side. South Korea is like our eyes and ears for this story. They are watching what their neighbor, North Korea, is doing. They are the ones who tell us important things about North Korea’s plans.

Big Countries Talking: What is Diplomacy?

That’s a long, funny word, isn't it? “Diplomacy!” It just means countries talking to each other. Imagine you and your friend want to play a game, but you both want to play different games. What do you do? You talk, right? Countries do the same thing! They talk to each other to solve problems, to share ideas, or to become better friends. This talking is super important because it helps keep the world a peaceful and happy place for everyone, including us!

North Korea Wants to Chat with the USA!

There is a very big and powerful country called the United States of America, or the USA for short. It's very far away from North Korea. Guess what? North Korea wants to talk to the USA! Why do they want to talk? Maybe they want to become better friends. Maybe they want to solve some old problems they had. Talking is always a good way to try and make things better, don't you think? Did you know? The USA is a very big country, so big that it has many different kinds of weather, from hot deserts to snowy mountains, all in one country!

Being Careful: What Does 'Wary' Mean?

Our friends in South Korea told us that North Korea is being “wary.” That’s another interesting word! Wary means being very careful. It means you are watching everything, looking left and right, just like when you cross a busy road. North Korea wants to talk to the USA, but they are being careful. They are thinking about their words and their actions very, very carefully. It's like when you are about to try something new, you are excited, but also a little bit careful, right?

Why Be Careful When Talking?

Sometimes, countries have had disagreements in the past. Like when friends have a small fight, they might still be a little careful when they talk again. It's normal to be careful when you want to make sure everything goes well. North Korea is trying to make sure their talks with the USA go smoothly and help everyone. Being careful means they want to get it right. It's like preparing for a big school play – you practice and are careful to remember your lines! Did you know? Even though they are neighbors, North Korea and South Korea have had some tough times in the past. That's why talking, even being careful talking, is so important for peace!

What Does 'Renew Diplomatic Dialogue' Mean?

This is a super fancy way of saying “start talking again.” “Renew” means to start something over, or to do it again. Like renewing a library book so you can read it more. And “dialogue” just means talking between two or more people, or in this case, countries! So, North Korea wants to *start talking again* with the USA. They’ve talked before, and now they want to pick up the conversation. It’s like when you stop playing a game for a little while and then decide to start playing it again with your friends.

What About Other Friends, Like Iran?

The grown-up news said something about another country called Iran. Don’t worry too much about the grown-up news! What it means is that North Korea is being careful about *all* its friends right now. Imagine you want to play a special new game with one friend. You might politely keep a little distance from other friends for a short while, just so you can focus on making that new game a success. It doesn’t mean you don't like your other friends, it just means you are focusing on something important. Keeping "at arm's length" means being polite but not super, super close right now. It's a way for North Korea to show the USA that they are very serious about their talks.

Why Is This Important for All of Us?

When big countries talk and try to solve problems, it's good news for the whole world! When countries are friends and talk nicely, there is less chance of fighting. Less fighting means more peace. And peace means everyone can live safely and happily, no matter where they are – even here in Africa! These talks help make our big Earth a safer and friendlier place for all the kids and grown-ups everywhere. It’s like when everyone in your classroom gets along and plays nicely; it makes school a happy place! Did you know? When countries talk and work together, they can even help each other with things like sharing food, medicine, or even building new schools! It’s called international cooperation.

So, What Did We Learn Today?

We learned that North Korea wants to talk again with the USA. They are being very careful and thoughtful while they do this. South Korea is watching and sharing this news with us. All this talking is super important for making our world peaceful and friendly. So next time you see a friend, remember how important it is to talk and listen! Keep shining, my amazing friends, and I’ll see you next time for more exciting news!
summary: North Korea wants to start talking again with the USA and is being very careful, as reported by South Korea, in hopes of making things better for everyone.
whyMatters: It matters because when big countries talk and try to be friends, it helps make the whole world a more peaceful and safe place for everyone, including us!
